commit.message:
web-gl implementation effects

* feat(webgl): add pure WebGL2 distortion grid background

Pure WebGL2 implementation — no Three.js, only raw API calls and GLSL 300 ES
shaders. Renders thin black lines on a white background with:

- Stretched grid: 30×30 lines with power-curve spacing (1.3 exponent) —
  lines denser at edges, wider at center for perspective effect
- Mouse distortion: vertices displace away from cursor with aspect-corrected
  gaussian falloff (radius 0.35, strength 0.12), smoothly lerped each frame
- Breathing animation: subtle sine oscillation (0.3% amplitude) keeps the
  grid alive without mouse interaction
- Each line subdivided into 80 segments for smooth vertex displacement

Files:
- src/lib/webgl/gridScene.ts: shaders, mesh generation, render loop,
  resize handling, visibility pause, full cleanup
- src/components/scenes/WebGLGrid.jsx: React island wrapper (client:load)
- maintenance.astro: WebGLGrid placed as first child in BaseLayout,
  position: fixed; z-index: -1; pointer-events: none

* chore(ai): add WebGL Copilot instruction file scoped to lib/webgl and WebGL scenes

.github/instructions/webgl.instructions.md — project-specific guide for
GitHub Copilot, scoped via applyTo to front/src/lib/webgl/** and
front/src/components/scenes/WebGL*.

Written from scratch (the auto-generated skill was discarded because it
used WebGL 1 GLSL 100 syntax, JavaScript instead of TypeScript, and
generic patterns that don't match this project).

Covers: WebGL2-only policy, GLSL 300 ES required syntax, scene module
pattern (init → render loop → cleanup), shader compilation helpers,
VAO/VBO geometry generation, mouse distortion pattern, React island
wrapper convention, performance rules, and common pitfalls.

* fix(three): make Spline scene background transparent to show WebGL grid

Change setClearColor from opaque purple (0x0d1030, 1) to fully transparent
(0x000000, 0). The renderer already has alpha: true, so the canvas now
composites over the white distortion grid behind it.

* fix(webgl): make grid perfectly uniform with equal-size squares

- Remove power-curve spacing from generateGridVertices — lines are now
  uniformly distributed (t * extent instead of sign(t) * |t|^1.3 * extent)
- Remove stretch warp from vertex shader (was bending lines outward)
- Remove breathing animation from vertex shader (was pulsing line positions)
- Only the mouse distortion remains — lines are perfectly straight at rest

* style(webgl): denser grid (80×80), wider distortion radius, tighter extent

* refactor(ai): reorganize Copilot instructions into skills-based structure

- Consolidate copilot-instructions.md with concise repo-wide rules
- Remove old webgl.instructions.md from .github/instructions/
- Add .github/skills/webgl-expert/ knowledge base

* feat(webgl): magnifying glass with brand blue border, split shaders/mesh/utils

- Separate shaders into shaders/grid/vertex.glsl and fragment.glsl
- Extract shaderUtils.ts (compile/link) and gridMesh.ts (geometry)
- Add magnifying glass zoom effect inside mouse circle (1.5x)
- Add brand blue (#3200f2) circle border via second draw call
- Add generateCircleVertices for ring geometry
- Enable alpha blending, brighter lines inside the lens
- Add env.d.ts declaration for *.glsl?raw Vite imports
- Add file-architecture pattern and distortion-grid example to webgl skill
- Update SKILL.md and task-routing.md with new references

* feat(maintenance): floating CTA with collision detection and responsive sizing

- Add floatingCta.ts: bouncing CTA that reflects off viewport edges,
  Spline card, marquee, and popin obstacles with AABB collision
- Expose card screen bounds via window.__getCardScreenBounds() from
  initSplineScene for real-time collision checks
- Scale down 3D card by 15% (camera push-back) to accommodate CTA
- Wire floating CTA init in maintenance.astro with card-bounds polling
- Responsive CTA sizing below 1200px (clamp 4rem–7rem)

* feat(skills): add WebGL shader animation agent, skills & GLSL utilities

- Add webgl-motion-architect agent for GPU-accelerated simulations
- Add webgl-shader-animation skill with comprehensive reference
- Add shader-animation patterns (physics, particles, noise, effects)
- Add reusable GLSL: noise, SDF-2D, easing, color utilities
- Update WebGL expert skill index with new references
- Add .mcp.json to .gitignore for API key security

* feat(agent): add Copilot setup steps and WebGL skill training capability

commit.message:
[AgentServer] Builder lifecycle ownership: each builder owns its full event lifecycle (#58094)

* Refactor builder lifecycle: each builder owns its full event lifecycle

Each content/refusal builder now owns its complete lifecycle from
EmitAdded() through EmitDone(), matching the Python SDK pattern where
parent builders auto-track children instead of manually tracking
completed content.

Changes:
- TextContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitTextDone(string?) for
  output_text.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done; tracks
  deltas and annotations internally; adds Annotations property
- RefusalContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitRefusalDone(string)
  for refusal.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done
- OutputItemMessageBuilder: remove EmitContentDone() overloads;
  AddTextContent/AddRefusalContent auto-track children; EmitDone()
  builds content from tracked children
- OutputItemReasoningItemBuilder: remove EmitSummaryPartDone();
  AddSummaryPart auto-tracks children; EmitDone() builds from
  tracked children
- Update TextResponse, all tests (29 files), samples, and docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename stale test methods to match new builder API names

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace BufferedStream+ArrayPool with zero-copy mmap PCAP reader

MmapPcapReader uses MemoryMappedFile for zero-copy I/O:
- Packet data points directly into mmap'd pages (no allocations, no copies)
- madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) enables aggressive kernel read-ahead
- Segmented Memory<byte> views handle files >2GB (1GB segments)
- UnmanagedMemoryManager bridges unsafe pointers to Memory<byte>

TimestampMergedReplayer simplified:
- Removed ArrayPool lifecycle tracking (_previousRented, ReturnRented)
- Mmap data is valid for replayer lifetime — no buffer management needed
- FeedHandler.EnqueueCopy no longer needs deep copy

Performance (52M packets, 15.7M orders, 3,639 books):
- Before: ~29s (BufferedStream + ArrayPool)
- After:  ~25s cold cache, ~20s warm cache (2.6M pkts/s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix print engine offset for non-zero viewBox origin elements

Forge-exported elements have a tight viewBox with non-zero x/y origin
(e.g. '85 196 930 494'). The print engine was computing the inner
transform offset as -w/2, -h/2 without subtracting the origin, causing
elements to shift down/right in print vs workspace.

Fix: offX = -vb[0] - vb[2]/2, offY = -vb[1] - vb[3]/2

Native elements (0 0 w h origin) are unaffected.

commit.message:
feat: add `client-id` input and deprecate `app-id` (#353)

GitHub now recommends using a GitHub App's Client ID for authentication.
This PR adds a first-class `client-id` input, keeps `app-id` available
for compatibility, and makes the migration path explicit in both runtime
behavior and documentation.

### Action inputs

- Adds a new `client-id` input
- Removes `required` from `app-id`
- Marks `app-id` as deprecated in `action.yml`

### Runtime behavior

- Updates input parsing to prefer `client-id`
- Falls back to `app-id` for existing workflows
- Adds a clear error when neither `client-id` nor `app-id` is provided

### Docs

- Updates the README to recommend `client-id`
- Switches usage examples to `client-id`
- Documents that `app-id` is deprecated and that `client-id` takes
precedence if both are set

### Regression coverage

- Adds a focused test proving a client-ID-shaped value works through the
new `client-id` input
- Adds coverage for the missing-ID validation path
- Updates snapshots to lock in the new metadata and runtime behavior

### Resulting usage

Users can migrate to the new input name directly:

```yaml
- uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v3
  with:
    client-id: ${{ vars.GITHUB_APP_CLIENT_ID }}
    private-key: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
```

---------

commit.message:
fix(czds): register SIGTERM handler before initial sync to prevent zombie containers

The _shutdown function was defined as a closure inside main() and only
registered AFTER run_catchup_cycle() completed. During the initial multi-hour
catchup cycle, SIGTERM had no registered handler — leading to zombie containers
that survived Docker rolling deploys indefinitely.

Fix:
- Extract _shutdown to module level (mirrors openintel_ingestor.py pattern)
- Register signal.signal(SIGTERM/SIGINT) at the very start of main(), before
  run_catchup_cycle(), so Docker's stop_grace_period actually works
- Reference global _scheduler_ref instead of the closed-over local 'scheduler'

This means future docker stack deploy calls will reliably stop old containers
within the stop_grace_period window (now 30m in stack config).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: lock_timeout=1 (1ms) to actually fail immediately on DDL contention

PostgreSQL lock_timeout='0' DISABLES the timeout (waits forever),
which is the default behavior — exactly what we were trying to avoid.
The correct value is lock_timeout=1 (1ms) which causes the DDL to fail
immediately if ACCESS EXCLUSIVE is unavailable, preventing the cascade
stall pattern where queued DDL blocks all subsequent INSERTs.
